steps.title: Find the visual spectacle signal
steps.content: The decisive signal is gorgeous display of nature paired with vivid visual imagery—”verdant sheen,” “festoons,” “resplendent” colors—all describing what Mr. Bradford is conscious of seeing.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Display” means an exhibition or presentation—something shown to be seen. Mr. Bradford is conscious of nature's gorgeous visual exhibition, so “exhibition” directly expresses this meaning. The other three either contradict showing something or don't apply to describing visual appearance.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
